metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and name (with ticket ID) the 'dependent floor-split story' referenced in What bullet 5 and AC4, or add it as a `deps` entry on this ticket so the executor can route the `ac_unverifiable` corpus evidence to the correct story.
evidence: Plan text: 'Record findings relevant to the ac_unverifiable hard-floor split on the dependent story (item 2) so its design starts from measured data'
Ticket deps array contains only one entry: `{target_id: '0730-6ceb-88c3-430c', relation: 'discovered_from'}` — a closed eval task
Search for 'floor-split' and 'floor split' returned no matches in the repo
AC4: 'A comment on the dependent floor-split story records the replay/corpus evidence about ac_unverifiable hard-floor fires' — no story is named or linkable
findings.finding: The plan references 'the dependent story (item 2)' for recording `ac_unverifiable` hard-floor findings (AC4), but neither a ticket ID nor a title for this floor-split story is given in the plan text. The ticket's single `deps` entry (`0730-6ceb-88c3-430c`) is a closed eval task unrelated to any floor-split story, and no floor-split dependency is discoverable.
findings.location: What section, bullet 5 and AC4: 'Record findings relevant to the ac_unverifiable hard-floor split on the dependent story (item 2)' / 'A comment on the dependent floor-split story records...'
scenarios: An executor cannot fulfill AC4 without knowing which story to comment on — the deliverable is unroutable.
If the floor-split story does not yet exist, AC4 is blocked by an undeclared prerequisite.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step specifying how to obtain the 'skipped remainder' count required by AC1 — either by modifying `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` to emit `skipped=N` alongside the segmented corpus line, or by prescribing an explicit pre-scan command (e.g. `grep -c '"impact_model_version"' .tickets-tracker/*/*-REVIEW_RESULT.json`).
evidence: AC1: 'the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder)'
What bullet 1: 'record the segment size vs total'
load() continues on mismatch with no counter: `continue  # segment by version; a missing tag (None) never equals a requested version`
main() prints only: `f'corpus: {len(revs)} sidecars / {len(by_ticket)} tickets / {sum(...)} findings'` — no skipped count
findings.finding: The calibration script's `load()` silently discards non-matching sidecars with no counter, so `main()` cannot emit the 'skipped remainder' that AC1 and What-bullet-1 require. The plan says to 'record the segment size vs total' and AC1 requires '(and the skipped remainder)', but the instrument doesn't produce that count without modification or a separate pre-scan step. The plan does not specify how the implementer is to obtain this number.
findings.location: What bullet 1 / AC1 / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py load() lines 40-64
scenarios: Implementer runs the script and gets segment size but no skipped count; AC1 cannot be satisfied without an extra manual step that the plan doesn't specify.
If the plan-v2 segment is small (e.g. <30 sidecars) and the skipped count isn't recorded, the reviewer cannot assess whether the corpus is truly non-trivial relative to the total.
findings.suggested_fix: In `load()`, add a skipped counter and return it alongside `by_ticket`; update `main()` to print `[segmented: N kept, M skipped (different/untagged version)]` on the same line as the corpus summary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `skipped` counter to `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py load()` and print it in `main()` so the AC#1-required 'skipped remainder' is produced automatically, OR explicitly add a manual corpus-scan step in the What section that computes the skipped count separately and records it.
evidence: AC#1: 'the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder)'
load() line 51: `continue` with no skipped-counter increment
main() lines 87-91: only prints len(revs), len(by_ticket), sum-of-findings — no skipped count
findings.finding: AC#1 requires the replay comment to include 'the skipped remainder' (sidecars excluded by the segment filter), but calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py has no counter tracking skipped sidecars — the load() function silently drops non-matching entries with a bare `continue` and no accumulator, and main() prints only the matched-segment stats. The required output field has no generator in the referenced tool.
findings.location: AC#1 / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py lines 44-51, 87-91
scenarios: Executor runs the script and records output; the 'skipped remainder' field is missing from the printout, making AC#1 literally unverifiable as written.
A reviewer checking the comment against AC#1 cannot confirm what fraction of the corpus was excluded.
findings.suggested_fix: In load(), add `skipped = 0` before the loop and `skipped += 1` on the `continue` branch, then return `(by_ticket, skipped)`. In main(), unpack and print `skipped` alongside the corpus line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'missing-oracle' and 'under-specified-oracle' as concrete, measurable categories (e.g., by referencing specific ac_unverifiable sub-types or verifier binary answers), and specify what sidecar field or derivation step distinguishes 'floor-driven block' from 'organic high-priority block' — or narrow AC#4 to what the existing sidecar schema actually supports.
evidence: AC#4: 'how often the 0.85 floor drove the block, missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix if derivable'
Search for 'missing-oracle' and 'under-specified-oracle' across entire repo: zero matches
calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py: findings loop reads criteria, decision, validity, priority — never reads verification.severity_attributes or ac_unverifiable value
decide.py line 145: _PLAN_HARD_OVERRIDE_FLOOR = 0.85 — the floor is applied in impact_plan, not stamped as a distinct sidecar field distinguishing it from organic high-priority findings
findings.finding: AC#4 requires measuring 'how often the 0.85 floor drove the block' and the 'missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix', but neither concept is defined in the plan, the script, or anywhere in the codebase — 'missing-oracle' and 'under-specified-oracle' are terms whose vocabulary is never specified. Additionally, the calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py script never reads severity_attributes (including ac_unverifiable values) from the sidecar findings, so it cannot derive whether the hard-floor (not raw verifier priority) was the operative cause of a block.
findings.location: AC#4 / What bullet 5 / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py
scenarios: Executor tries to compute 'floor-drove-block' fraction but has no field in the sidecar that distinguishes impact_plan=0.85-via-floor from impact_plan=0.85-via-natural-severity — both look the same in the replay.
Executor cannot produce the 'missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle' breakdown because those categories are undefined.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a skipped-sidecar counter to `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py`'s `load()` (or `main()`) so the script emits the total corpus size and skipped count alongside the segment counts, satisfying AC #1's 'skipped remainder' requirement.
evidence: AC #1 text: 'the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder)'
load() code: `continue  # segment by version; a missing tag (None) never equals a requested version` — no skipped counter accumulated
main() output: `corpus: {len(revs)} sidecars / {len(by_ticket)} tickets / … findings` — only post-filter counts emitted; no total or skipped tally
Evidence class: Verified (observed) — the script was read in full and no skipped-count accumulator exists
findings.finding: The script `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` cannot produce the 'skipped remainder' count required by AC #1: `load()` silently skips non-matching sidecars with no counter, and `main()` prints only the post-filter segment size. The AC requires both the segment counts AND the skipped remainder to be recorded, but the named tool has no implementation path to emit the latter.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC #1; docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py lines 41-51, 87-91
scenarios: Executor runs the script and records its output; the comment satisfies the segment counts but cannot satisfy the 'skipped remainder' sub-requirement, leaving AC #1 partially unmet.
A future reviewer checking whether the corpus was large enough to matter cannot determine what fraction of sidecars were excluded by the version filter.
findings.suggested_fix: In `load()`, accumulate `skipped = 0` and increment it on each `continue`; return `(by_ticket, skipped)` and have `main()` also print `total={len(revs)+skipped}, skipped={skipped}` alongside the segment line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the threshold-edit target in the What section from `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` to `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (the file that actually holds `block_threshold` values and is already correctly named in the ticket's `file_impact` field).
evidence: Plan text: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/) citing the replay evidence'
`src/rebar/llm/criteria/` contains only `__init__.py`, `ids.py`, `model.py`, `overlay.py` — no block_threshold data
Thresholds confirmed in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (e.g., G1G2 block_threshold: 0.7 at line 726)
Ticket's own `file_impact` field correctly names `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`
findings.finding: The plan's edit target for threshold changes is named as `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` (the shared criteria machinery layer), but that directory contains no `block_threshold` values — the actual thresholds live in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`. The ticket's own `file_impact` field correctly names `criteria_routing.json`, but the plan body diverges, pointing an executor to the wrong directory.
findings.location: What section, bullet 4: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/)'
scenarios: An executor following the plan literally would search `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` for threshold fields and find none, causing confusion or incorrect edits.
A future plan-review agent grading G1G2 on this ticket would flag the named edit target as hallucinated.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the bullet to: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json`) citing the replay evidence; if no change is warranted, record the no-change decision with rationale.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the edit-target path in What bullet 4 from `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` to `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (where block_threshold values actually live).
evidence: Plan: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/)'
list_directory('src/rebar/llm/criteria'): __init__.py, ids.py, model.py, overlay.py — no block_threshold values
src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json: contains all block_threshold values (0.6, 0.7, 0.75, 0.95) for plan-review criteria
findings.finding: The plan names `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` as the edit target for applying warranted threshold changes (What bullet 4, AC#3), but confirmed inspection shows that directory contains only ids.py, model.py, and overlay.py — none of which hold block_threshold values. The actual threshold registry is src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json. This is an instruction-locality error: the referenced edit location resolves to the wrong file.
findings.location: What section bullet 4 / src/rebar/llm/criteria/ / src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json
scenarios: An executor follows the plan literally and looks for block_threshold values to edit inside src/rebar/llm/criteria/ — finds none and is confused about where to make the change.
A reviewer checking the Gerrit diff looks in the wrong directory to verify the change was applied.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'non-trivial' with a quantitative floor (e.g., 'segment must contain ≥ MIN_N=25 sidecars overall; if below, record the count and defer all threshold changes').
evidence: 'verify...that the segmented corpus is non-trivial; record the segment size vs total' — 'non-trivial' is undefined
MIN_N=25 is stated for per-criterion deferral but not applied as the corpus-level gate
No percentage floor or absolute count stated for the overall segment
findings.finding: 'Non-trivial' segment size used as a go/no-go gate in the first What bullet has no quantitative floor, making it unverifiable: an executor cannot determine when the segment is large enough to proceed.
findings.location: What section, first bullet: 'verify...that the segmented corpus is non-trivial'
scenarios: Segment has 30 sidecars total — is that non-trivial? Executor has no rule to apply.
findings.suggested_fix: 'verify that the segmented corpus contains ≥ 25 sidecars; if below this floor, record the count and defer all threshold changes per the MIN_N deferral rule.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the required output when the mix is NOT derivable: e.g., 'if the mix cannot be distinguished from corpus data alone, record that explicitly in the comment along with a raw count of ac_unverifiable hard-floor fires, so the dependent story at minimum has the fire-rate evidence.'
evidence: 'A comment on the dependent floor-split story records the replay/corpus evidence about ac_unverifiable hard-floor fires (how often the 0.85 floor drove the block, missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix if derivable)' — the 'if derivable' clause leaves the output undefined for the non-derivable case
No fallback deliverable stated when the mix cannot be computed
findings.finding: AC4 contains the hedge 'if derivable' for the missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix, making the criterion conditionally satisfiable without specifying the required deliverable when the mix is NOT derivable — the done state is ambiguous.
findings.location: AC4: 'missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix if derivable'
scenarios: The corpus data does not include enough signal to distinguish missing-oracle from under-specified-oracle — executor has no stated output to produce, leaving the dependent story with no evidence base.
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC4: '...missing-oracle vs under-specified-oracle mix where derivable from sidecar metadata; where not derivable, record that distinction as unknown and state the raw ac_unverifiable hard-floor fire count and rate.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the 'raise' decision rule co-located with the adjudication protocol — e.g., define the signal thresholds (validity drop below X, or rev_rr below Y) that trigger a threshold increase from the current value, mirroring the precision the 'lower' direction already gets from the script's BLOCK-ELIGIBLE branch.
evidence: Plan: 'for each blocking-tier criterion … compare the replay proposal to the current threshold and decide keep/raise/lower with rationale'
Script proposal block (lines 227-241): classifies as LOW-DATA / FP-PRONE / BLOCK-ELIGIBLE / ADVISORY-KEEP — no 'raise' classification or threshold-increase trigger
The 'raise' branch is a referenced decision outcome with no specified trigger condition in either the plan or the script
findings.finding: The adjudication decision rule for the 'keep/raise/lower' outcome (What bullet 3) references signals (validity, rev_rr, p90, fire rate, n vs MIN_N) but never specifies the protocol for the 'raise' direction: under what signal combination should a currently-0.60 criterion be raised to 0.70 (or higher)? The script's proposal logic only emits BLOCK-ELIGIBLE/FP-PRONE/LOW-DATA/ADVISORY-KEEP — it has no 'raise existing threshold' branch. The adjudicator is left without a co-located rule for one of the three named outcomes.
findings.location: What section, bullet 3 / docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py lines 219-241
scenarios: The replay shows a currently-0.60 criterion with degraded signals (lower validity or lower rev_rr post-model-change); the adjudicator has no protocol for deciding whether to raise it.
Two independent executors produce different 'raise' decisions because the rule is unspecified.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step to record the calibration-3 adjudication rationale durably: either update ADR-0036 with a 'Calibration 3 outcome' section, add a CHANGELOG entry in `src/rebar/llm/criteria/`, or commit inline comments to each changed threshold file — so the decision is discoverable without finding the ticket.
evidence: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/) citing the replay evidence' — citing is promised but only in a ticket comment, not in a durable artifact
'if no change is warranted, record the no-change decision with rationale' — again, only in a comment
No mention of updating ADR-0036, a criteria-registry CHANGELOG, or inline comments in registry files to capture the calibration-3 decision
findings.finding: The adjudication rationale (why each threshold was kept/raised/lowered) is captured only as a ticket comment, which is ephemeral and non-discoverable for future maintainers. If thresholds change, engineers later inspecting the criteria registry have no durable, co-located record of the decision basis. The plan calls for Gerrit-merged registry changes but names no update to ADR-0036, a CHANGELOG, or inline registry comments that would make the decision auditable without hunting down the ticket.
findings.location: AC line 3 ('Either warranted threshold changes are landed in the criteria registry … OR a no-change decision is recorded in the adjudication comment') and 'Apply warranted changes' in What section
scenarios: A future engineer sees threshold 0.63 in the registry and cannot determine whether it was intentional, which calibration run set it, or what the replay evidence was, without archaeological ticket search.
ADR-0036 mandated version-segmented replay; after this run the ADR has an open 'result' that is never back-filled, making the ADR document incomplete.
findings.suggested_fix: In each modified registry file, add a comment block such as `# Calibration 3 (YYYY-MM-DD): lowered from 0.70 -> 0.63; replay n=NN, validity=X, rev_rr=Y, p90=Z — see ADR-0036 §Calibration-3`. Also add a 'Calibration 3 Results' subsection to ADR-0036 summarising the replay outcome and adjudication decisions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Cite the grounded source (sidecar scan output, log, or ticket comment) that supports the 'block rate ~25% -> 52-67%' claim, or replace with a placeholder to be filled by the corpus scan step.
evidence: Searched for 'block rate rose', 'block rate 25%', 'block rate 52%', '25% -> 52%' — zero matches across the entire repo.
ADR-0036 and `docs/plan-review-threshold-calibration.md` record calibration history but no post-plan-v2 block-rate measurement.
The plan cites this statistic to establish severity/urgency of the calibration work; an unverifiable premise here could misframe the adjudication.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Block rate rose ~25% -> 52-67% immediately after' the plan-v2 model shipped, but no grounded source for this claim exists anywhere in the codebase, docs, or linked artifacts. It is stated as an established fact motivating urgency, yet no telemetry log, comment, doc, or ticket records it.
findings.location: Plan description paragraph: 'Block rate rose ~25% -> 52-67% immediately after.'
scenarios: The block rate increase could be overstated or understated, leading to incorrect urgency framing and potentially skewing the adjudication decision (raise/lower/keep).

If the 52-67% range is wrong, the plan's conclusion that the 0.85 floor 'has never been replayed' may be the only valid motivator — which is already covered — making the block-rate claim decorative but misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the block-rate claim from the description into the 'What' step: add a sub-bullet under the corpus scan step to 'record the pre-vs-post block rate from the sidecar corpus scan' so the number becomes an output of this ticket rather than an unverified input premise.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the expected `impact_model_version` tag value (or enumerate the valid alternatives) and add a decision rule: e.g., 'if the tag is absent or differs, use the closest match and record the discrepancy; if the segment is empty, defer per MIN_N=25.'
evidence: 'verify first, via a corpus scan, what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry' — investigation deferred to executor
No branch or fallback stated for the case where the tag value differs from the assumed 'plan-v2' string
The script invocation uses `--impact-model-version plan-v2` but this flag value depends on the corpus scan outcome
findings.finding: The 'verify first, via a corpus scan, what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry' bullet is a placeholder investigation step with no stated decision rule: if the tag value differs from 'plan-v2', the executor has no specified path (abort? use closest match? update the script flag?). This is a 'verify whether' pattern that defers a real design choice.
findings.location: What section, first bullet: 'verify first, via a corpus scan, what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry'
scenarios: Tag is stored as 'v2', 'plan_v2', or a date string rather than 'plan-v2' — executor must stop and decide whether to change the flag or abandon the task
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the open-ended corpus scan with a stated default: 'Expect tag value `plan-v2`; if the corpus scan reveals a different value (e.g. `v2`), use that value as the `--impact-model-version` argument and record the actual tag in the output comment. If no plan-v2 sidecars are found at all, record the finding and defer threshold changes.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC1 an explicit output requirement: record the confirmed `impact_model_version` tag value(s) found in the sidecar corpus and verify it matches the assumed 'plan-v2' identifier before the replay counts are accepted.
evidence: What says: 'verify first, via a corpus scan, what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry'
AC1 says: 'the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder)' — tag-value confirmation is absent from AC1 outputs
findings.finding: The What section (bullet 1) describes a mandatory pre-step — confirming what `impact_model_version` tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry and that the segmented corpus is non-trivial — but no AC verifies this confirmation was done; AC1 only captures counts, not the tag-value verification outcome.
findings.location: What bullet 1 vs AC1
scenarios: The script is run against the wrong corpus segment (e.g., sidecars tagged differently than expected), producing misleading replay data, and no AC catches the mismatch.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1 to: 'A comment records (a) the confirmed tag value(s) found via corpus scan, (b) the segment's sidecar/ticket/finding counts (and the skipped remainder), (c) the per-criterion table, and (d) the script's proposal block.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for criteria registry threshold changes (e.g., Gerrit revert procedure, or a note that the prior threshold values are preserved in the commit history and can be reverted within N hours if block-rate monitoring flags regression).
evidence: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/) citing the replay evidence'
AC3: 'threshold changes are landed in the criteria registry (change merged via Gerrit)'
Block rate context: 'Block rate rose ~25% -> 52-67% immediately after' — indicates these thresholds directly control production block behavior
No rollback or revert mechanism mentioned anywhere in the plan
findings.finding: The plan applies potentially significant changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/) that affect downstream block-rate behavior, but no rollback or back-out path is specified for the case where the new thresholds prove incorrect in production.
findings.location: What bullet 4; AC3
scenarios: A threshold is lowered based on replay data, but the replay corpus was not representative; block rate drops below acceptable safety levels in production with no documented revert path.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Rollback' note to the What or AC3: 'If post-merge monitoring shows block-rate regression beyond ±X%, revert the Gerrit change within 24 h and re-open adjudication.' Also record the pre-change threshold values explicitly in the adjudication comment so reversion is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a failure-mode section specifying what to record and do if the corpus scan finds a trivial/empty plan-v2 segment or if the calibration script errors (e.g., record the failure as a ticket comment, state the deferral rationale, and define the retry/escalation path).
evidence: Plan says 'verify first, via a corpus scan, what impact_model_version tag value plan-v2 sidecars actually carry, and that the segmented corpus is non-trivial' but specifies no action if the corpus is trivial or the script errors.
No retry, fallback, or partial-output handling is mentioned anywhere in the plan.
All four ACs presuppose a successfully completed script run with valid output.
findings.finding: The plan has no specified error handling or fallback for failure of `calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` or the preliminary corpus scan (e.g., script error, unreadable sidecars, empty segment, or MIN_N<25 for all criteria). All four ACs depend on the script producing valid output, but there is no stated procedure for what to record or decide if the run fails or produces degenerate results.
findings.location: What section — 'Run calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py ...' bullet; AC line 1
scenarios: Script errors midway through corpus scan; ticket stalls with no recorded outcome and no defined next step.
Segment turns out to have N<25 for every blocking criterion; MIN_N deferral kicks in for all, but the plan doesn't say what to record or whether the ticket is closed or deferred.
Sidecar files are unreadable/malformed; script produces partial output; adjudication is done on incomplete data silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Failure handling' bullet: 'If the script fails or the plan-v2 segment is below MIN_N=25 for all blocking criteria, record the error output and segment size as a ticket comment, declare the replay deferred pending corpus growth, and re-open at next calibration cycle. Do not apply threshold changes from a failed or degenerate run.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify which services/components consume `src/rebar/llm/criteria/` thresholds, how they pick up changes (hot-reload / deploy / restart), and confirm no concurrent threshold-change PRs will be in flight during this merge.
evidence: Plan says changes are 'merged via Gerrit' but says nothing about consumers picking up the new values.
No mention of whether the criteria registry is loaded at startup, polled, or hot-reloaded by the sidecar/review service.
Block rate impact is the stated motivation; if consumers don't reload on merge, the threshold change has no effect until some unspecified action is taken.
findings.finding: The plan mutates shared criteria registry state (`src/rebar/llm/criteria/`) consumed by the live plan review system, but does not enumerate the consumers of those thresholds or specify how/when the change takes effect (hot-reload on merge, requires a deploy, requires a service restart). Without this, operators cannot confirm the block-rate change is actually live after the Gerrit merge.
findings.location: What section — 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds (src/rebar/llm/criteria/)' bullet; AC line 3
scenarios: Thresholds are merged but the running service still uses the old in-memory values; block rate remains at 52-67% and the team incorrectly concludes the calibration had no effect.
A concurrent threshold change is also in Gerrit; merge order determines final state with no concurrency control called out in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address the ADR-0036 §4 A/B gate in the plan: either (a) confirm no plan-review labeled fixture exists and record this as a known gap with a rationale for proceeding on replay-only evidence, or (b) add a step to create/use a minimal plan-review labeled set to run the A/B gate.
evidence: ADR-0036 §4: 'A threshold-down is proposed ONLY when this gate passes AND the segmented field corpus shows the separation holds at scale.'
`docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py` exists and is the ADR-0036 gate; its fixture is code-review only (`code_review_impact_labels.jsonl`).
ADR-0036 Consequences: 'A checked-in plan-review labeled set, if added later, plugs into the same A/B shape.'
The plan's What/AC sections reference 'replay signals (validity, rev_rr, p90, fire rate, n vs MIN_N)' but do not mention `ab_impact_model.py` or a plan-review labeled set.
No plan-review labeled fixture found in `tests/unit/fixtures/` — only `code_review_impact_labels.jsonl` exists.
findings.finding: The plan says threshold changes are applied 'if the replay warrants them,' but ADR-0036 §4 mandates BOTH the segmented replay AND passage of the A/B gate (`docs/experiments/ab_impact_model.py`) before a threshold-down is justified. The plan omits the A/B gate step entirely. ADR-0036 acknowledges no plan-review labeled fixture exists yet, but the plan does not address this gap nor does it explain why the A/B gate is skipped for plan-v2.
findings.location: What section: 'Apply warranted changes to the criteria registry thresholds... if the replay warrants them'; ADR-0036 §4 (Calibration cadence, step 4).
scenarios: If threshold changes are applied based on replay signals alone without acknowledging the missing A/B gate, the plan implicitly contradicts its governing ADR and creates a precedent for skipping the gate in future calibrations.
A future reviewer comparing this ticket's outcomes against ADR-0036 will find the A/B step undocumented, making auditability impossible.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the 'Adjudicate' bullet: 'Note: the ADR-0036 A/B gate (ab_impact_model.py) has no plan-review fixture; per ADR-0036 Consequences, replay-only adjudication is the approved path until a labeled set is added. Record this explicitly in the adjudication comment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit step (or AC condition) specifying that any 'lower' adjudication decision must first confirm the ADR-0036 step-4 A/B gate requirement is satisfied — either by running `ab_impact_model.py` against a plan-review labeled fixture (if one exists), or by recording a documented exception/rationale for why the dual-gate is inapplicable to this plan-review-specific calibration.
evidence: ADR-0036 step 4: 'A threshold-down is proposed ONLY when this gate passes AND the segmented field corpus shows the separation holds at scale.'
What bullet 3: 'decide keep/raise/lower with rationale, honoring the MIN_N=25 low-data deferral' — no A/B gate guard mentioned.

AC2: 'every blocking-tier criterion mapped from current threshold to a keep/raise/lower decision with rationale grounded in the replay signals (validity, rev_rr, p90, fire rate, n vs MIN_N)' — no A/B gate condition listed.
ab_impact_model.py scores plan-review threshold decisions but uses code-review fixture format; however, ADR-0036 step 4 applies to both gates' calibration cadence.
findings.finding: ADR-0036 cadence step 4 mandates that 'A threshold-down is proposed ONLY when this gate passes AND the segmented field corpus shows the separation holds at scale' — requiring the A/B gate (`ab_impact_model.py`) to pass before a lowering decision. The plan's adjudication step (What bullet 3 and AC2) says to 'decide keep/raise/lower with rationale grounded in the replay signals' but makes no mention of the A/B gate as a precondition for any lower decision. This means the plan's mechanism allows threshold-lowering on replay evidence alone, potentially violating the ADR's dual-gate requirement.
findings.location: What bullet 3 / AC2 / docs/adr/0036-impact-model-rollout-and-calibration-cadence.md step 4
scenarios: Replay shows rev_rr >= 0.6 and mv >= 0.55 for a criterion, triggering a BLOCK-ELIGIBLE proposal and a threshold-lower decision — but the A/B separation gate was never run, violating the ADR's data-gated requirement.
A future auditor reviewing the threshold change finds no evidence the ADR-0036 step-4 gate was cleared, creating a process-compliance gap.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the What section whether the ADR-0036 A/B gate step (`ab_impact_model.py`) is skipped for this calibration (because no plan-review labeled set exists), and if so, record a substitute objective criterion or a deferral decision — do not leave it as an unacknowledged gap.
evidence: ADR-0036 §4: 'A threshold-down is proposed ONLY when this gate passes AND the segmented field corpus shows the separation holds at scale.'
ab_impact_model.py line 35: `_DEFAULT_FIXTURE = 'tests/unit/fixtures/code_review_impact_labels.jsonl'` — no plan-review equivalent fixture exists in the repo.
ADR-0036 Consequences: 'A checked-in plan-review labeled set, if added later, plugs into the same A/B shape.' — confirming the gap is known but the plan does not address it.
ABSENCE: neither the What section nor any AC mentions running `ab_impact_model.py` or justifies why the A/B step is inapplicable for this calibration.
findings.finding: The plan implicitly skips the A/B gate step (ADR-0036 §4: `ab_impact_model.py`) when adjudicating threshold changes for plan-v2, but never acknowledges or justifies this omission. The A/B tool only supports the code-review fixture; no plan-review labeled set exists. If the replay warrants a threshold-down, the plan's adjudication prose becomes the sole gate — an unresolved deviation from the ADR-0036 cadence that the plan author asserts is safe but does not state.
findings.location: ## What (adjudication bullet) and AC line 3 ('Either warranted threshold changes are landed…')
scenarios: Replay warrants lowering a threshold; the adjudication comment approves it; there is no A/B gate to catch a miscalibrated `impact_plan` regression — exactly the hazard ADR-0036 was written to prevent.
A future auditor reviews the calibration record and cannot determine whether the A/B step was intentionally skipped, forgotten, or deemed inapplicable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a bullet: 'Note: the ADR-0036 A/B gate (ab_impact_model.py) applies only to the code-review fixture; no plan-review labeled set exists. This calibration therefore relies on the segmented-replay signals alone. A threshold-down requires all three replay signals to be favorable (mean validity ≥ X, rev_rr ≥ 0.60, n ≥ MIN_N=25) as the substitute objective criterion, and the decision is recorded as provisional pending a future plan-review labeled-set A/B.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
